title: Implement a fixed-timestep loop with interpolation
description: Build the accumulator loop that steps simulation at fixed dt and interpolates rendering, with a clamp against the spiral of death.
body: Steps: 1. Each frame, measure real elapsed time and add it to an accumulator. 2. While accumulator >= dt, run one fixed simulation step and subtract dt. 3. Clamp the number of steps per frame (or cap accumulated time) so a slow frame cannot trigger runaway catch-up (the spiral of death). 4. Compute alpha = accumulator/dt and interpolate render state between previous and current sim states. 5. Render using the interpolated state. Rollback point: before swapping a variable-delta loop (it changes timing behavior). Validation: movement is identical across frame rates and rendering is smooth without judder; a loop that scales movement by raw frame delta has not met the fixed-timestep requirement.
